info.title: u22a8.ai API · v1
info.summary: Versioned, resource-shaped REST API for scoring models.
info.description:
Versioned REST surface for u22a8.ai. Resource shape: `/v1/models/{handle}/...`. One error envelope, request id on every response, cursor-based pagination, OpenAPI 3.1.

## Authentication

Every endpoint requires a valid API key — `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Issue keys in the platform console. A key authors only in namespaces it owns and reads its own namespaces plus the public ones (`u22a8` / `live` / `bench`). The token-free public scoring surface is `/m/<handle>` (see the top-level API); within /v1, only `/v1/health` and the docs are open.

get.description: DB connectivity check — same probe as the top-level ``/health``.

List models, ordered by handle. Returns a cursor-paginated page even when the result fits — clients should treat the shape as stable and opaque-cursor.

Without ``namespace``, the listing is scoped to the public ``u22a8`` namespace. Pass ``namespace=<name>`` to list elsewhere — it must be a namespace your key owns, or one of the public ones. Filter by ``state`` (``draft|busy|ready|failed|archived``) and ``kind`` (``scoring`` only at launch).

post.description: Create a draft model. ``handle`` is required and must be in a namespace your key owns — there is no auto-minting. ``kind`` is reserved-now and only accepts ``scoring`` at launch.

get.description: Returns the model's structured detail (JSON) by default. Set `Accept: text/markdown` to fetch the markdown **model card** instead — the same card MCP `resources/read` returns for this URI, so the advertised resource URI is directly fetchable. Not consent-gated: the card is how a caller reads a model's terms.

delete.description: Soft delete — moves the model to ``state='archived'``. Samples, version history, and the row itself are preserved (restorable by an operator). Idempotent: deleting an already-archived model returns 200. Hard delete is reserved for account-deletion flows and not exposed on the REST API at this time.

get.description: Trait metadata with sample counts, descriptions, and polarity labels.

post.description: Add or replace traits on a model, keyed by ``key`` (or derived from ``name`` when ``key`` is omitted). Scoring-kind only.

Fit intrinsic trait axes (PCA/LDA) from the model's labeled samples and attach them as ``kind='intrinsic'`` traits (DR 035). Draft-only. Returns 202 + run id on enqueue; the model enters ``busy`` (``last_operation.kind='discover'``) and returns to ``draft`` with the new traits visible on ``GET /traits`` at completion. 409 + ``X-Run-Id`` on an idempotency hit (same sample set + effort).

get.description: One trait's metadata, including the DR 035 fields — ``kind`` and, for discovered intrinsic traits, ``discrimination`` / ``explained_variance`` / ``anchor_sample_ids``. ``key`` matches by trait key or name.

post.description: Append samples to a model. Each sample's ``trait`` field is resolved by key or name. Scoring-kind only.

get.description: Cursor-paginated sample listing. ``trait`` filters by key or name.

post.description: Lifecycle entry point (DR 032). From ``draft``: cold-start (bundling + synthesis + Bayesian sweep + fit). From ``ready`` + ``reoptimize_params=true``: fresh sweep. From ``ready`` without the flag: no-op — buffered retrains run automatically. Returns 202 + run id on enqueue, 409 + ``X-Run-Id`` on idempotency hit.

Score content against the model's traits. URLs in `content` are auto-fetched and their extracted text is scored. Plain-text bodies (curl convenience: pipe a string or URL with no JSON wrapper) are accepted alongside JSON. Set `Accept: text/plain` for the bar-chart formatter.
Score two texts (`a` is baseline, `b` is candidate) and return the per-trait delta plus the overall improvement. URLs in `a` and `b` are auto-fetched.

post.summary: Fetch a URL and extract its text
post.description: Resolve a URL to clean text. Reuses the same fetch + readability extractor as the score endpoint. Returns a content hash for dedup. Used by the SDK importer to resolve URL sources at import time so training data stays deterministic.

get.description: List a model's param snapshots, newest first. Cursor is the smallest ``version_no`` from the previous page.
Resolve a ``"vN"`` or tag name to its full snapshot. The ``snapshot`` field carries per-trait axes/artifacts and is heavy; for metadata-only access, use the list endpoint instead.

post.description: Pin ``{name}`` to the model's current active version. Re-using a tag moves the pointer. Tag names matching ``v\d+`` are rejected to keep the ``vN`` namespace unambiguous.

put.description: Swap ``models.current_version_id`` to the resolved version. Non-destructive: samples continue to accumulate against the latest sample set; buffered retrains continue to mint ``vN+1`` regardless of which version is currently active.

traits.title: Traits
traits.description: Score only these traits. Omit to score all.

handle.title: Handle
handle.description: Namespaced handle, ``<namespace>.<name>`` (e.g. ``u22a8.foo``). Required — there is no auto-minting. The namespace must be one your key owns. Want a random handle? Generate the string yourself.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Model kind. Only ``scoring`` is accepted at launch — future kinds (classification/conformance/retrieval) land additively.

zero_data_retention.title: Zero Data Retention
zero_data_retention.description: Per-model zero data retention opt-in (DR 032 § ZDR). When True, samples are purged at every training completion, the buffered retrain worker skips this model, and ``POST /score { feedback }`` is rejected.

CreateModelRequest.title: CreateModelRequest
CreateModelRequest.description:
``POST /v1/models`` body. ``handle`` is required and must be in a
namespace the caller's key owns (U22-20).

effort.title: Effort
effort.description: DR 031 effort dial — ``low|medium|high|xhigh|max``. Decoded into discovery knobs (trait cap, interpretation budget) by ``oa/training/effort.py``; ``null`` falls through to ``DEFAULT_EFFORT``. No per-knob overrides (DR 035 §5).

DiscoverRequest.title: DiscoverRequest
DiscoverRequest.description:
``POST /v1/models/{handle}/traits/discover`` body (DR 035). Effort-only.

Draft-only. Pools the model's labeled samples, fits intrinsic trait axes
(PCA/LDA), and attaches them as ``kind='intrinsic'`` traits. Response is
the shared :class:`TrainResponse` (202 + run id on enqueue, 409 +
``X-Run-Id`` on idempotency hit) — discovery is just another async
lifecycle transition.

url.title: Url
url.description: HTTP(S) URL to fetch and extract.

content_sha256.title: Content Sha256
content_sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 of the UTF-8 bytes of `content`. Stable for dedup.

fetched_at.title: Fetched At
fetched_at.description: UTC timestamp captured at fetch time.

state.title: State
state.description: Lifecycle state (DR 032 amended by DR 035): ``draft|busy|ready|failed|archived``. ``busy`` is the generic transient state for any async operation — see ``last_operation.kind`` for the mechanism.

current_version_id.title: Current Version Id
current_version_id.description: BIGINT id of the ``model_versions`` row whose snapshot currently serves scoring. NULL pre-first-train. Updates atomically on every successful train/retrain. (Pre-U22-28 this field carried a TEXT run id; clients pinned to the old shape need to update.)

version_no.title: Version No
version_no.description: Monotonic per-model version number of the active snapshot (the integer behind ``vN``). NULL pre-first-train.

version_tags.title: Version Tags
version_tags.description: User-named tags pointing at the active version.

last_operation.title: Last Operation
last_operation.description: Most recent lifecycle transition (DR 035): ``{kind, status, started_at, run_id?, progress?, error?}``. ``kind`` names the mechanism behind a ``busy`` state (``train|discover|retune|retrain_buffered|diagnose``). NULL on models that have never had a lifecycle operation.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; null when exhausted.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Page size used for this response.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; null when exhausted.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Page size used for this response.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; null when exhausted.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Page size used for this response.

quality.title: Quality
quality.description: Sample quality in [0, 1]. 1.0 = positive pole, 0.0 = negative pole.

version_id.title: Version Id
version_id.description: Active version that served this score, formatted as ``vN``. Null when the model has never been trained.

sample_ids.title: Sample Ids
sample_ids.description: Sample row ids minted from the ``feedback`` block, in the trait order supplied. Empty when no feedback was sent.

ScoreMetadata.title: ScoreMetadata
ScoreMetadata.description:
Audit / lifecycle metadata attached to a score response (DR 032).

``version_id`` is the active param-snapshot the score served from,
formatted as ``"vN"`` to match the pinned-handle form (``handle@vN``).
``null`` pre-first-train. ``sample_ids`` is populated only when the
request included a ``feedback`` block — one id per trait whose
feedback was captured.

effort.title: Effort
effort.description: DR 031 effort dial — ``low|medium|high|max``. Affects the Bayesian-sweep budget on cold-start and re-tune. Ignored for buffered retrains (no sweep).

reoptimize_params.title: Reoptimize Params
reoptimize_params.description: When True, force a fresh Bayesian sweep from a ``ready`` model. When False, ``ready`` is a no-op (buffered retraining handles the incremental update path).

TrainRequest.title: TrainRequest
TrainRequest.description:
``POST /v1/models/{handle}/train`` body. All fields optional.

From ``draft``: kicks off cold-start training (transitions to
``training``). From ``ready`` + ``reoptimize_params=True``: kicks off a
fresh Bayesian sweep. From ``ready`` without ``reoptimize_params``: no-op
(buffered retrains run automatically based on sample-add triggers).

run_id.title: Run Id
run_id.description: Newly enqueued run id, or the matching existing run id on an idempotent hit. ``null`` when ``state == 'ready'`` and no re-optimization was requested (no run was queued).

state.title: State
state.description: Current model state after the call.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: DR 035 definition mode: ``extrinsic`` (labels are the trait, axis re-derived every train) or ``intrinsic`` (axis is the trait, frozen across re-trains — produced by discovery).

discrimination.title: Discrimination
discrimination.description: Discriminator strength in [0, 1] for discovered traits (1.0 = perfect separation, 0.5 = chance). NULL for authored.

explained_variance.title: Explained Variance
explained_variance.description: Variance the intrinsic axis explains (PCA/LDA). NULL for extrinsic.

anchor_sample_ids.title: Anchor Sample Ids
anchor_sample_ids.description: Positive/negative exemplar sample ids anchoring an intrinsic axis (``{positive: [...], negative: [...]}``). NULL for extrinsic.

key.title: Key
key.description: Stable identifier. Derived from ``name`` if omitted (snake_case, alphanumerics + underscore).

zero_data_retention.title: Zero Data Retention
zero_data_retention.description: Toggle ZDR (DR 032 § ZDR). ``False → True`` purges existing samples atomically with the column update. ``True → False`` enables retention forward only — past purges are not reversed.

version_retention_policy.title: Version Retention Policy
version_retention_policy.description: Auto-prune policy override (U22-28). Keys: ``keep_unnamed`` (int, default 100), ``keep_first_of_month`` (bool, default True). NULL clears the override and reverts to defaults.

UpdateModelRequest.title: UpdateModelRequest
UpdateModelRequest.description:
``PATCH /v1/models/{handle}`` body. Missing fields = no change.

``state`` is intentionally **not** patchable (DR 035): lifecycle state
is owned by the FSM (``oa.store.lifecycle.transition``). Use ``POST
/train`` to move ``draft → ready`` and ``DELETE`` to archive. Unknown
fields (including ``state``) are rejected so the contract is explicit
rather than a silent no-op.

VersionActivateRequest.title: VersionActivateRequest
VersionActivateRequest.description:
``PUT /v1/models/{handle}/versions/active`` body.

Exactly one of ``version`` / ``tag`` must be set. ``version`` is the
monotonic ``vN`` form; ``tag`` is a user-named pointer.

snapshot.title: Snapshot
snapshot.description: Self-contained params blob — per-trait axes and artifacts. Heavy; omitted on scoring-scope reads.

VersionDetail.title: VersionDetail
VersionDetail.description: A version with its full snapshot payload (authoring scope).

tags.name: Scoring
tags.description: Score and compare text against models.

tags.name: Extract
tags.description: Fetch and extract text from URLs.
